text: Writing clean code is essential for both yourself and other developers who may work on your project. Readable code is much easier to understand and modify. One simple way to improve readability is to follow consistent naming conventions. For example, use descriptive names like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `calcTP`. It’s also a good idea to break long pieces of logic into smaller, focused functions. Each function should do one thing well. This approach makes your code more organised and easier to test. Ultimately, clean code saves time and effort, ensuring long-term maintainability for any software project.
